refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall minimize cycle time by optimizing load, travel, and dump phases to meet the customer-defined cycle time target.
refined_fr.rationale: Decomposing the overall cycle time KPI into sub-functions targeting each phase of the cycle allows focused design and verification of each segment, improving clarity and traceability.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure p95 cycle time for load, travel, and dump phases individually and in aggregate under defined duty cycle and site conditions.

candidate_dps: Optimize vehicle mechanical and control systems for load phase
Implement efficient autonomous or teleoperated travel control
Streamline dump mechanism and control for rapid unloading
selected_dp.selected_dp: Optimize vehicle mechanical and control systems for load phase; Implement efficient autonomous or teleoperated travel control; Streamline dump mechanism and control for rapid unloading

potential_sources: Interdependence between travel and load phases timing
Coordination between teleoperation commands and vehicle control
coupling_check.notes: DPs are logically separable by cycle phase but require interface coordination, especially between teleoperation and vehicle control.

assumptions_risks: Assumes teleoperation commands can be reliably integrated with vehicle control systems
Risk of timing delays due to network latency in teleoperation
Mechanical constraints may limit achievable cycle time improvements
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider explicit DP for teleoperation command optimization to reduce latency impact.

im_boundary.name: Vehicle
im_boundary.scope: Physical and control systems responsible for load, travel, and dump phases execution

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Receives commands and cycle phase instructions from Teleoperation module

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Sends cycle phase status and performance data to Teleoperation and Safety Services

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: Exchanges safety status and alerts with Safety Services

module_containment_scores.notes: Vehicle module contains core physical and control systems for cycle phases; low diffusion risk.
Teleoperation influences control commands but does not contain physical execution; higher diffusion risk if assigned.
Safety Services mainly monitors and alerts; minimal containment for cycle time functions.
NGHE Solution is a high-level system boundary; less suitable for detailed cycle time DP containment.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Assigning cycle time optimization DP to Vehicle module centralizes physical and control execution, minimizing diffusion and improving ownership clarity.
containment_risks: Potential interface complexity with Teleoperation commands
Risk of insufficient coordination with Safety Services impacting cycle time
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Define clear interface contracts and synchronization mechanisms between Vehicle and Teleoperation modules.

fr_dp_gate.reason: FR refinement clearly decomposes cycle time KPI into manageable sub-functions; selected DPs logically address each sub-FR with acceptable coupling.

diffusion_risks: Teleoperation commands may diffuse into Vehicle control logic if interfaces are not well defined.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Vehicle module is the best containment candidate for cycle time DP; interfaces with Teleoperation and Safety Services are well identified.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification can be performed by measuring cycle times and monitoring telemetry and safety alerts through defined interfaces.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear allocation of cycle time DP to Vehicle module with explicit interface boundaries.

recommended_revisions: Add explicit DP for teleoperation command optimization to reduce latency.
Strengthen interface definitions between Vehicle and Teleoperation modules.

decision.justification: While the decomposition and assignments are sound, adding an explicit DP for teleoperation command optimization will reduce risks related to network latency and improve cycle time performance.
next_level_sub_frs: Optimize teleoperation command latency and reliability

revise.why: To explicitly address teleoperation command optimization as a distinct design parameter, reducing coupling and diffusion risks.

assumptions: Teleoperation commands can be integrated with vehicle control systems.
Mechanical and control system improvements can achieve cycle time targets.
open_risks: Coordination complexity between Vehicle and Teleoperation modules.
evidence_needed: Latency measurements of teleoperation command transmission.
Cycle time performance data under integrated system operation.
